sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Still 5 green cells at the same positions. Clicking on (25,42) after selecting (19,36) didn't do anything.
Let me try a completely different approach. Maybe the game uses ACTION7 for something important. Let me try ACTION7.
Actually, let me first try to understand the game better by looking at the history of successful actions. The only action that changed the green cells was:
1. Select (19,24)
2. Click on g overlay at (19,12)
This removed all 5 green cells. But the level wasn't complete.
Maybe the goal is to have exactly 3 green cells at column 42 (like the previous successful merge sequence achieved). Let me try to reproduce that sequence:
1. Select (19,24)
2. Click on g overlay at (19,12) - this should merge (19,18) and (19,24) into (19,42)
But wait, clicking on the g overlay at (19,12) removed ALL green cells, not just (19,18) and (19,24).
Let me re-examine the previous successful sequence. Looking at the history:
- After selecting (19,36) and clicking on (19,33), a g overlay appeared
- After clicking on the g overlay, (19,18) and (19,24) were removed, and (19,42) was created
- This resulted in 3 green cells at column 42
But now when I try the same sequence, clicking on the g overlay removes all green cells.
The difference might be that the board state is different. In the previous sequence, the green cells were at different positions, or the g overlay was at a different position.
Let me try the merge sequence again, but this time I'll be more careful about the order and positions.
Actually, let me try a different approach. Let me try clicking on the g overlay that appears when I select (19,36). The g overlay is at (19,42). Let me click on it.
Wait, I already tried that and it didn't work. Let me try clicking on the g overlay at a slightly different position.
Actually, looking at the image, the g overlay is at (19, 42) which is in the top grid. But (19,42) is also where the bottom grid green cells are. Maybe the g overlay is at a different position.
Let me check the ascii output to find the exact position of the g overlay.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check ascii for G/g overlays after selecting (19,36)
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 19, 'col': 36}])

ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
for r in range(64):
    row = ascii_lines[r]
    special = [(i, c) for i, c in enumerate(row) if c in 'Gg']
    if special:
        print(f"Row {r} G/g: {special}")

# Also check segmentation for any non-N, non-gray nodes
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] not in ('w', 'g', 'G', 'N', 'p', 'B', 'S'):
        b = n['boundary']
        cr = sum(p[0] for p in b)//len(b)
        cc = sum(p[1] for p in b)//len(b)
        print(f"{n['color']}: center=({cr},{cc}), pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Try ACTION7
result2 = action([{'action': 'ACTION7'}])
print(f"Action7 result: {last_action_result}")

seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
green_nodes = [n for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"Green after ACTION7: {len(green_nodes)}")
for n in green_nodes:
    b = n['boundary']
    cr = sum(p[0] for p in b)//len(b)
    cc = sum(p[1] for p in b)//len(b)
    print(f"  center=({cr},{cc})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
